prompt_text: Nutshell vs Salesforce pricing and features
response:
Nutshell is generally **cheaper, simpler, and faster to adopt** for small–mid-size teams, while Salesforce is **far more powerful, extensible, and expensive**, aimed at complex, multi‑department organizations.[1][3][4]

Below is a concise side‑by‑side on **pricing and core features**.

---

## 1. Pricing comparison

Public list prices vary slightly by source and change over time, but the pattern is consistent: **Nutshell is much lower cost and more all‑inclusive**, while **Salesforce is higher cost with many paid add‑ons**.[1][2][3][5][6][9]

### Typical per‑user pricing tiers

| Tier level | **Nutshell** (approx per user/month, annual) | **Salesforce Sales Cloud** (approx per user/month, annual) |
|-----------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| Entry     | **$13–$22** (Foundation / Starter)[1][3]     | **$25** (Essentials / Starter)[1][2][3]                    |
| Mid       | **$25–$42** (Growth / Pro)[1][2][3]          | **$75–$100** (Professional / Pro)[1][2][3][9]              |
| Upper     | **$59–$79** (Business / Enterprise)[1][3]    | **$150–$350+** (Enterprise / Unlimited)[1][3][9]           |
| Top‑end   | N/A beyond ~$79[3]                           | Up to **$500–$550+** with advanced AI tiers[3][9]          |

Key pricing notes:

- **Nutshell**  
  - No setup or implementation fees listed; they emphasize *“no setup fees, implementation costs, or surprise charges”*.[3][5]  
  - Transparent per‑user pricing; support is included.[3][5]  
  - Common public tiers: Starter / Foundation, Growth, Pro, Business, Enterprise.[1][2][3]

- **Salesforce**  
  - Core small‑business plans: **Essentials $25**, **Professional $75**, **Enterprise $150**, **Unlimited $300** per user/month in one comparison.[1]  
  - Other sources list **Pro at ~$100**, Enterprise **$175**, Unlimited **$350**, and AI/Agentforce tiers at **$500+** per user/month.[3][9]  
  - Add‑ons such as Sales Engagement, AI, advanced analytics, and marketing automation often cost extra.[3][5][9]  
  - Premier support is typically **~30% of license fees**, plus potential **API overage and AppExchange add‑on fees**.[5]

### Total cost of ownership (TCO)

One detailed estimate comparing a **10‑person team** on mid‑market tiers (Nutshell Pro vs Salesforce Enterprise) shows:[3]

| Cost line (Year 1, 10 users) | **Nutshell Pro** | **Salesforce Enterprise** |
|------------------------------|------------------|---------------------------|
| Licenses                     | $5,040           | $21,000                   |
| Add‑ons                      | $0–$1,920        | ~**$9,000+**              |
| Implementation               | $0               | **$25,000–$75,000**       |
| Support                      | Included         | ~**$6,300**               |
| **Year 1 total**             | **$5,040–$6,960**| **$61,300–$111,300+**     |

So Salesforce can easily be **10–20× more expensive in Year 1** for a similar team size at common mid‑market tiers.[3]

---

## 2. Feature comparison (high‑level)

### Core positioning

- **Nutshell**
  - Focus: **simple, sales‑centric CRM with strong email marketing automation** for SMBs.[1][2][5]
  - Strengths: ease of use, quick rollout, sales pipeline and email tools in one place, good value.[1][2][4][5]

- **Salesforce**
  - Focus: **full enterprise CRM platform and ecosystem** (sales, service, marketing, commerce, analytics).[1][5][6]
  - Strengths: deep customization, automation, integrations, multi‑department workflows, massive app ecosystem.[1][3][5][6][9]

### Major functional areas

| Area | **Nutshell** | **Salesforce** |
|------|--------------|----------------|
| Contact & account management | Yes – straightforward contact, company, and activity tracking.[1][2][6] | Yes – very robust contact, account, and relationship hierarchies.[1][6]
